data.title: VanEck: The average put/call ratio for Bitcoin is 0.77, the highest level since June 2021.
data.description: Matthew, Head of Digital Asset Research at VanEck, wrote that Bitcoin entered a consolidation phase after a sharp drop. The 30-day average price fell by 19%, but spot prices stabilized, with realized volatility decreasing from 80 to 50 and futures funding rates dropping from 4.1% to 2.7%. The options market saw peak defensiveness, with the average put/call ratio at 0.77, the highest level since June 2021, and put premiums relative to spot volume reaching an all-time high of 4 basis points. On-chain activity and miner selling remained subdued, with transaction volume down 31%, daily fees down 27%, and the distribution rate to long-term holders slowing. Miners sold almost all newly issued BTC.

data.title: Polymarket adds weather forecast events for five Chinese cities including Beijing and Shenzhen.
data.description: Odaily Seer's monitoring shows that Polymarket has added weather forecast events for five Chinese cities: Beijing, Shenzhen, Chongqing, Chengdu, and Wuhan. Previously, Polymarket added weather forecast events for Shanghai and Hong Kong. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the forecast market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Central Banks Adopt Hawkish Stance Amid Energy Crisis Concerns
data.description: Analysts from Malayan Banking have indicated that a situation reminiscent of the 2022 energy crisis is once again influencing central bank decisions. According to Jin10, the repercussions of the conflict in Iran mirror those of the Russia-Ukraine conflict, leading to a sharp rise in energy prices. A series of central bank decisions this week highlights that policymakers are closely monitoring the current situation. Observations show that several major central banks, including the Reserve Bank of Australia and the Federal Reserve, have shifted towards a hawkish stance, resulting in significant declines in gold and silver prices. As central banks take action to stay ahead of the yield curve, another round of position adjustments may be underway. Many central banks faced criticism in 2022 for maintaining overly loose policies based on the expectation that the energy shock would be temporary. This time, central banks are adopting a more proactive approach.

data.title: STOCKS | China's Stock Market Sees Mixed Opening with Tech and Energy Sectors Leading Gains
data.description:
On March 20, China's stock market indices opened with mixed results, experiencing fluctuations throughout the day. According to Jin10, the Shanghai Composite Index turned positive near the close, while the ChiNext Index surged over 3%, reaching a new high for the year. The combined trading volume for the Shanghai and Shenzhen markets reached 1.44 trillion yuan, an increase of 139.2 billion yuan compared to the previous trading day, with over 3,200 stocks declining.

The optical module and CPO concept sectors saw significant gains, influenced by the overnight rise in U.S. optical communication stocks. Yuanjie Technology's stock price hit the 20cm limit, surpassing 1,000 yuan, while New Yisheng and Zhongji Xuchuang also experienced substantial increases. The power sector remained active, with Huadian Liaoning achieving a five-board streak and Shaoneng Shares a three-board streak.

The photovoltaic energy storage sector showed strong performance, driven by Tesla's procurement news, with companies like Chint Power and Shangneng Electric hitting the limit-up. Conversely, the oil and gas, and computing power leasing sectors faced declines, with Super Communication hitting the limit-down, followed by Zhunyou Shares, Potential Hengxin, Davi Technology, and Dongfang Guoxin.

data.title: STOCKS | CATL Shares Reach Record Premium in Hong Kong
data.description: Shares of CATL briefly reached a record premium in Hong Kong compared to their mainland-listed counterparts. Bloomberg posted on X that the surge was driven by robust earnings and energy supply disruptions, which increased demand for the battery manufacturer. CATL's performance highlights the growing interest in the company amid global energy challenges.

data.title: Macquarie Projects Potential Early Rate Hike by Bank of Japan
data.description: Macquarie analysts have indicated uncertainty regarding the timing of the Bank of Japan's next interest rate hike. According to NS3.AI, they project the next move could occur around July. However, the analysts noted that if the yen continues to weaken, there is a risk of an earlier rate increase.

data.title: Indian Rupee Hits Record Low Against US Dollar at Opening
data.description: The Indian rupee opened at a historic low against the US dollar, trading at 92.89, marking a 0.28% decline. According to Jin10, this drop reflects ongoing concerns about the country's economic stability and external pressures impacting currency valuation. Analysts suggest that factors such as global market volatility and domestic economic challenges are contributing to the rupee's depreciation. The currency's performance is closely monitored by investors and policymakers, as it may influence India's trade dynamics and inflation rates.

data.title: Bluesky Secures $100 Million Series B Funding to Enhance App Development
data.description: Bluesky has announced the completion of a $100 million Series B funding round, led by Bain Capital Crypto. According to NS3.AI, the funding round was finalized in April 2025 but has only been disclosed now. The company plans to utilize the capital to expand its team and further develop the Bluesky app and ATProto.

data.title: Polymarket's probability that "FDV's market capitalization will exceed $1 billion one day after EdgeX opens" has fallen to 21%, with a 24-hour drop of 30%.
data.description: According to Odaily Seer's monitoring, the probability of EdgeX's FDV exceeding $1 billion market capitalization one day after its opening on Polymarket has dropped to 21%, a 30% decrease in the past 24 hours, with a trading volume of nearly $2.5 million. Last night, EdgeX announced that its EDGE airdrop terms page is now live. According to its website, the EDGE airdrop application period is from 18:00 Beijing time on March 19th to 7:59 on April 1st. Furthermore, Binance has launched EdgeX EDGE token contract trading, with a temporary price of $0.67 and a temporary FDV of $670 million. Odaily Seer continues to monitor the prediction market, seeing changes before pricing.

data.title: Farrer Park Considers Asset Sale in Singapore
data.description: Farrer Park is reportedly considering the sale of several assets, including a hospital, medical center, and hotel in Singapore. Bloomberg posted on X, citing unnamed sources familiar with the matter. The potential sale reflects ongoing evaluations of asset portfolios in the region. Further details about the sale process or potential buyers have not been disclosed. The move comes amid a broader trend of asset reallocation in the healthcare and hospitality sectors.

data.title: Japan's Central Bank Faces Uncertain Timing for Next Move Amid Middle East Conflict, Analysts Say
data.description: According to Jin10, Macquarie analysts have highlighted the increased uncertainty surrounding the timing of the Bank of Japan's next policy move due to ongoing Middle East conflicts and fluctuating energy prices. This week, the central bank opted to keep interest rates unchanged while signaling openness to tightening policies in the coming months. Governor Kazuo Ueda noted that rising crude oil prices could worsen trade conditions, hinder economic growth, and elevate inflation expectations. The Macquarie team anticipates that the Bank of Japan will exercise patience during this period of uncertainty, suggesting that the next interest rate hike might occur in July. However, there is a risk of earlier action, especially if the yen continues to depreciate.

data.title: Walrus Token Scheduled for Major Unlock on March 27
data.description: Walrus (WAL) is set to unlock approximately 38.833 million tokens on March 27 at 10:00 AM UTC+8. According to ChainCatcher, this significant token release is part of the planned schedule by the Web3 asset data platform RootData. The upcoming unlock is expected to have a notable impact on the market dynamics of the Walrus token.

data.description:
In the last 24 hours, several tokens have seen significant net inflows, according to ChainCatcher. Leading the list is TENDIES, which experienced a net inflow of $3,000 and surged by 198%, currently priced at $0.0001.

AIFRUITS follows with a net inflow of $2,000, marking a 52.5% increase, and is now valued at $0.0008. IMAGINE also saw a net inflow of $2,000, with an impressive rise of 2088.2%, maintaining a price of $0.0001.

The token 我的刀盾 recorded a net inflow of $2,000 but faced a decline of 12%, with its current price at $0.0033. Lastly, GPD had a net inflow of $1,000, achieving a growth of 181.3%, and is priced at $0.0001.

data.title: Goldman Sachs Revises UK Interest Rate Forecast
data.description: Goldman Sachs has updated its forecast for the UK interest rates, predicting that the Bank of England will maintain its current rates throughout 2026. According to Jin10, this marks a change from their previous expectation of quarterly rate cuts starting in July. The financial institution now anticipates that the Monetary Policy Committee will begin to gradually lower the benchmark rate in 2027, eventually reaching 3%.

data.title: Macquarie: The timing of the Bank of Japan's interest rate hike remains unclear, with risks leaning towards an earlier pace.
data.description: Macquarie analysts say the timing of the Bank of Japan's next move is more uncertain than ever, given the uncertainties surrounding the Middle East conflict and energy prices. This week, the central bank chose to keep interest rates unchanged, while leaving the door open for further tightening in the coming months. Governor Kazuo Ueda pointed out the risk that rising oil prices could worsen terms of trade, drag down the economy, and push up inflation expectations. The Macquarie team expects the Bank of Japan to remain patient during this period of uncertainty, believing the next rate hike is likely in July. Nevertheless, the risk lies in acting sooner, especially if the yen continues to depreciate. (Jinshi)

data.title: Decentralized social network Bluesky announced today that it has completed a $100 million Series B funding round, led by Bain Capital Crypto.
data.description: Decentralized social network Bluesky announced today that it has completed a $100 million Series B funding round, led by Bain Capital Crypto and completed in April 2025, though this was not previously disclosed. Other investors participating in this round include existing investors Alumni Ventures and True Ventures, as well as Anthos Capital, Bloomberg Beta, and Knight Foundation. Previously, Bluesky completed a $15 million Series A funding round led by Blockchain Capital (expected to close in 2024) and raised $8 million in seed funding the previous year from Neo and other angel investors. Bluesky did not disclose its latest valuation. The new funds will be used to expand Bluesky's team, while the company continues to develop the Bluesky app and its underlying technology, ATProto, which currently powers a range of applications within Atmosphere—a large open social ecosystem containing approximately 20 billion public records, including posts, likes, comments, and other interactions. (techcrunch)

data.title: Australia Considers Windfall Tax on LNG Industry Amid Rising Prices
data.description: Prime Minister Anthony Albanese has directed Australia's Treasury to explore the possibility of implementing a windfall tax on the nation's extensive liquefied natural gas (LNG) sector. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the government's intention to benefit from the surge in fuel prices. The move comes as Australia seeks to leverage its natural resources amidst global economic shifts. The proposed tax aims to ensure that the country gains from the increased revenues generated by the LNG industry, which has seen significant price hikes recently. This initiative reflects the government's strategy to optimize economic benefits from its natural gas reserves, aligning with broader fiscal policies.

data.title: Phishing Attack Results in Theft of $200,000 in USDC and wmtUSDT
data.description:
A phishing attack has led to the theft of approximately $200,000 in USDC and wmtUSDT, according to ChainCatcher. The incident involved a malicious Permit and Approve transaction signed by an address beginning with 0x9709.

GoPlus has advised users to carefully verify transaction details and contract addresses when signing any on-chain authorizations or offline signature requests. This precaution is essential to prevent assets from being stolen through unauthorized or malicious requests.

data.title: Standard Chartered and BSI Denied Participation in 1MDB Winding-Up Applications
data.description:
Standard Chartered and BSI have been unsuccessful in their attempts to join winding-up applications related to the Malaysian sovereign wealth fund 1MDB, as ruled by Singapore's High Court. Bloomberg posted on X, detailing the court's decision, which marks a significant development in the ongoing legal proceedings surrounding 1MDB.

The case involves complex financial transactions and alleged misappropriation of funds linked to 1MDB, which has been under scrutiny for several years. The High Court's ruling prevents Standard Chartered and BSI from being part of the legal process aimed at dissolving entities connected to the fund.

This decision is part of broader efforts to address the financial irregularities associated with 1MDB, which have led to investigations and legal actions across multiple jurisdictions. The involvement of major financial institutions like Standard Chartered and BSI highlights the international scope of the 1MDB scandal.

The ruling underscores the challenges faced by financial entities in navigating legal frameworks when dealing with cases of alleged financial misconduct. As the situation unfolds, it remains to be seen how this will impact the broader efforts to resolve issues related to 1MDB.

data.title: SBI Narrows Valuation Gap with HDFC Bank Amid Growth Stock Demand
data.description: State Bank of India (SBI) is closing the valuation gap with HDFC Bank, driven by increased demand for growth stocks and premium alcohol brands. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the competitive dynamics between these major financial institutions. The shift in investor interest towards growth stocks is contributing to SBI's improved market position. Additionally, the rising popularity of premium alcohol brands is influencing market trends, further impacting the valuation landscape. As these factors continue to evolve, the financial sector is witnessing significant changes in investment patterns.
